page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server belonging to the ecs.c7a instance family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7T83 processor running at 2.55 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance provides 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation without hyperthreading sharing. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding 2.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates on the KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.c7a.2xlarge demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% of tested systems. Multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and ray tracing benchmarks fall within the average tier. Memory read bandwidth is average, but large-block memory write bandwidth is weak, placing in the bottom 25%. The instance achieves average performance in database operations, static web serving, and small-model LLM text generation, though prompt processing for medium-sized models is weak.

Given its compute-to-memory ratio and lack of local storage, the ecs.c7a.2xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or high-speed local disk I/O. It is well-suited for workloads such as web serving, software compilation, and light database operations. It is less optimal for memory-write-intensive applications or large-scale language model inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC 7T83 processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 2.55 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, though multi-core performance and memory read bandwidth remain average. Large-block memory write bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, ranking in the bottom 25%. This hardware profile is suited for compute-focused tasks such as web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations, while being less efficient for memory-write-heavy applications or large-scale language model inference.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong single-core CPU performance for compute-bound workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and strong single-core performance for compute-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7T83 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores
Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, requiring network-attached storage
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and light database operations
